hypothesis: If a Transformer-based encoder-decoder model is trained with a joint spectral reconstruction loss on IR spectra of mixtures spanning binary to ternary compositions and varying concentration ratios, then it will accurately identify the constituent molecules and reconstruct their pure-component spectra from the mixed spectra (achieving high Top-1/5/10 accuracy and Tanimoto similarity) because the reconstruction objective forces latent representations to align with pure-component spectra and disambiguate overlapping spectral features.
